text: We are now days into the war on Iran and reports are emerging that the US–Israeli strategy is shifting
Not towards peace but to armed ethnic insurrection and possible civil war
Let me explain what we know and why this is happening
Reports including from Kurdish sources suggest the CIA is arming Iranian Kurdish separatist groups
Those groups are based in the mountains of northern Iraq and western Iran
Those forces are preparing to launch an uprising against the Iranian state with US help
There are several Iranian Kurdish groups with thousands of fighters
They oppose the Islamic Republic and want an independent Kurdish state
There have been signs in the last weeks that something was escalating
In February Kurdish opposition parties formed a new alliance against the Islamic Republic
In the past seventy two hours the US and Israel bombed Iranian military positions in western Iran
Analysts say this may be paving the way for a Kurdish advance
Iran has also been bombing Kurdish positions in Iraq
Donald Trump spoke with Kurdish leaders about the war and what may come next
US Secretary of State Marco Rubio indicated the war will intensify
Officials leaving a classified briefing expressed concern about the next phase
Some fear US ground forces may become involved
So what is the point of all this
Defence analysts say there are several possible outcomes
An uprising could occupy Iranian forces in the northwest
It would stretch Iran’s military across another front
It could trigger unrest in major Iranian cities
That unrest could attempt to overthrow the Islamic Republic
It could even lead to the break up of Iran
There are also Balouch separatists along the Iran Pakistan border
Tehran and Islamabad have increased cooperation to fight armed groups there
Both countries say regional actors are exploiting tensions
So why try to ignite an ethnic civil war
So far there have been no major protests against the Islamic Republic
There have also been no defections from the armed forces
Analysts say Iran appears able to fight a long war
They believe Iran could exhaust US resources and those of its allies
Iran is capable of producing large numbers of missiles each month
Iran is not a weak or fragmented state
About forty percent of the population comes from different ethnic groups
Iranians are strongly nationalistic
Analysts believe ethnic uprisings would not gain wide support
Even from many opponents of the government
The only certainty may be more Iranian bloodshed
And two nuclear powers pulling the strings
